evidence_quotes: high amount of currencies in games
make it mandative to disclose rates for games and such kind of things when money are involved
it's hard to read TOS and EULA
it shouldn't be allowed to change them retroactively after we already payed for a product
make it clear when it's a rent and when it's a purchase;

paragraphs.text: In short: help us avoiding dark patterns from companies such as high amount of currencies in games (an example of it is Blue Protocol, where tens of currencies make it hard to understand that they can't be converted into the premium currency and a credit card is needed for the best stats and exclusive clothes and so on); regulate free market by preventing private companies from censoring legal content for moral, political or religious reasons, since such kind of activities are discriminating people; make impossible to deny payments for matters of taste, politic, religion and so on as long as its not proven to be illegal; make illegal for private companies to implement malware for preventing people from downloading apps from unofficial sources, since we purchesed our devices (an example of that is what Google want to do with its app store); make parent control optional and never mandative, because it's parents responsability to look after their kids and it's insane to punish people without kids because of their lazy parents: there are already several ways to prevent kids from accessing dangerous materials, but the parents don't use them and they complain when it's too late; avoid implementing digital IDs, since it's dangerous for privacy and easy to hack, but another problem is that poor people is already cut off by many services because some of them are avaiable only online, usually from apps that require a smartphone and can't even run on a old pc or other devices, a way to phisically book appointment, such the medical ones, is required; make it mandative for teachers to follow courses about cybersecurity and to teach about it in school, so everyone will be more safe; make it mandative to disclose rates for games and such kind of things when money are involved (many new games already comply to this, but most of older games such as Elsword doesn't, even if sometimes unofficial or untranslated lists are avaiable); reduce the bureaucracy, especially the online one and make it less hard to understand contracts and checking our stuff on both private and "public" sites and apps; when we buy a product we should have the right of download it and keep using it and modify it as much as we want, so we need a way to prevent companies from destroying our purchased products (such as movies, games, books and so on); generally speaking, it's hard to read TOS and EULA and it shouldn't be allowed to change them retroactively after we already payed for a product (there is no option to deny them without giving up on our alredy payed products, and we have no option such as keep using the product only offline in case we don't agree); make it clear when it's a rent and when it's a purchase; essentially everything is a rent online, but it keep saying "buy" instead of "rent", which is unfair and misleading; make it illegal to brick phisical products by making them work only with a remote control panel from a cloud, which is not guaranteed to last and require us to keep paying for internet in order to use our purchased products (such as washing machines, fridges and so on); make it illegal to add ads inside our previously purchased products (such as cars and fridges); payment processors are now needed for everything because we shifted from the physical money, to the digital currency, so they should no prevent anyone from opening a bank account or transfering money, as long as it is not proven to be illegal, which should lead to a report to the police and not to an aribitrary block; ways to prevent scams are needed as well, but we also need more freedom to speech since big private platforms can still censor us and a free debate is needed and shouldn't be censored; companies shouldn't be allowed to deactivate an account without a clear reason as well, but TOS and EULA usually make us accept that they can do it without refounding us and this is usually not disclosed before the purchase; we need less online, more physical products in the end.
